steps.title: Use the roots
steps.content: The function is in factored form. Its roots are $x=2$ and $x=-15$.

steps.title: Find the midpoint
steps.content:
The parabola opens upward, so its minimum occurs at the vertex. The vertex is halfway between the two roots.

$$\frac{2+(-15)}{2}=\frac{-13}{2}$$

steps.title: Calculator shortcut
steps.content: Graphing $f(x)=(x-2)(x+15)$ shows the vertex at the same input value. The answer is -13/2.
